creator: amdmi3@FreeBSD.org
text:
(In reply to George from comment #6)

> The blog post at
> https://blog.torproject.org/blog/tor-0275-released-and-stable is entitled
> "Tor 0.2.7.5 is released and stable," so formally it *is* stable.

Download page still lists 0.2.7 branch as unstable.

> OTOH, the 0.2.7.x branch requires a new version of OpenSSL than is on the
> 9.x FreeBSD branch, and there is no new 'alpha' branch from the Tor Project
> yet, so for port categorization purposes, it makes sense to keep
> security/tor at 0.2.6.x and security/tor-devel at 0.2.7.x IMHO.

I'd stick to that for stability purposes for the time being. 0.2.7 is only gaining usage and may still reveal some bugs. When dust settles, we can switch tor port to it and remove tor-devel completely.
